title: Display-Name Registry Schema (Canonical Column-to-Label Mapping)
description: Canonical contract for `data/display_name_registry.csv` — the cross-pair mapping from parquet canonical column names to chart-ready display labels and units. Governed by DATA-D13 (producer rule) and META-CF (Contract File Standard). This schema describes the row shape of the CSV; the CSV file itself is the canonical instance Vera and Ace consume directly. Closes the Wave-5 audit finding that display_name was mandated by DATA-DD1 (data dictionary Display Name column) but had no portfolio-level single source of truth; drift was inevitable when the same indicator appeared in multiple pair dictionaries. Cross-references: DATA-D5 (per-parquet sidecar which cross-validates against this registry), DATA-D12 (column-suffix linter — the registry is also the canonical suffix registry for display-name governance), META-XVC (cross-version consistency for the same column name), VIZ-A2 (axis-label unit match), APP-AF3 (metric interpretation rule).

schema_version.description: Semver of this instance's conformance to the registry contract. Bumped when the row shape is materially restructured (add/remove/rename a column of the CSV).

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 date-time at which Dana last regenerated the registry.

columns.description: One entry per canonical parquet column name across the entire portal. The CSV-on-disk instance has these exact fields as its column headers in the order (column_name, display_name, unit, axis_label). Duplicate `column_name` values are prohibited — a canonical name has exactly one display mapping, forever.

column_name.description: Canonical parquet column name in snake_case, with DATA-D12 unit suffix where applicable (e.g. `hy_ig_spread_bps`, `spy_ret_1d`, `vix_idx`). Matches the EXACT string as it appears in the parquet file. Unique across the registry.

display_name.description: Human-readable chart-title-style label suitable for portal headers and KPI cards. Includes the unit in parentheses where ambiguity is possible. Example: `HY-IG Credit Spread (bps)`.

unit.description: Controlled unit vocabulary (mirrors DATA-D2 and data_subject.schema.json). Must match the `unit` field in the per-parquet DATA-D5 sidecar; mismatches are a cross-artifact integrity failure.

axis_label.description: Short chart-axis label (typically 2-6 words). Shorter than `display_name` because axis real estate is limited. Example: `Spread (bps)` or `Return (%)`. Vera's VIZ-A2 axis builder consumes this field.
